title: Map Builder — Editor Visual de Mapas

userStories.title: Extract expanded Emerald tiles
userStories.description:
Extract ~40 tiles from the Emerald exterior tileset into organized subdirectories.

Source: `public/assets/items/sprite-options/emerald-exterior-tileset.png` (600K, 16×16 grid)

Create script `scripts/extract-editor-tiles.mjs` using sharp (`npm i -D sharp`, remove after).

Create directories:
- `public/assets/tiles/emerald/ground/`
- `public/assets/tiles/emerald/water/`
- `public/assets/tiles/emerald/nature/`
- `public/assets/tiles/emerald/buildings/`
- `public/assets/tiles/emerald/decoration/`

Tiles to extract (16×16 each, identify by scanning tileset visually):

**ground/**: grass-light.png, grass-dark.png, grass-flower.png, dirt.png, sand.png, path.png, city-floor.png
**water/**: deep.png, shallow.png, edge-n.png, edge-s.png, edge-e.png, edge-w.png
**nature/**: tree.png, tree-large.png (32×32 multi-tile), bush.png, rock.png, fence-h.png, fence-v.png, stump.png
**buildings/**: pokecenter.png (48×32), mart.png (32×32), house.png (32×32), gym.png (48×48)
**decoration/**: flowers.png, sign.png, lamppost.png, mailbox.png, bench.png

The Emerald tileset is ~600px wide. Divide by 16 to get grid columns (~37). Analyze the tileset:
- Top rows: grass variants (green hues)
- Middle rows: terrain (dirt, sand, paths)
- Water section: blue tiles with edge transitions
- Tree section: dark green multi-tile canopies
- Building section: structured rectangular tiles
- Use hardcoded pixel coordinates with comments.

Also keep the existing 9 tiles in `public/assets/tiles/emerald/` (grass-light.png through path.png) — these are used by the current game. The new tiles go in subdirectories.

After script runs: `npm uninstall sharp`.

userStories.title: Download and extract FRLG tiles
userStories.description:
Download the FireRed/LeafGreen exterior tileset and extract ~35 tiles.

Source: Check if `public/assets/items/sprite-options/frlg-tileset.png` exists. If not, download from The Spriters Resource or use the pokemonAutoChess repo.

Alternative source (pokemonAutoChess): Look for Kanto/FRLG tilesets at:
`https://raw.githubusercontent.com/keldaanCommunity/pokemonAutoChess/master/app/public/src/assets/tilesets/`

If no FRLG tileset available, search for it at: `https://www.spriters-resource.com/game_boy_advance/pokemonfireredleafgreen/`

Create directories:
- `public/assets/tiles/frlg/ground/`
- `public/assets/tiles/frlg/water/`
- `public/assets/tiles/frlg/nature/`
- `public/assets/tiles/frlg/buildings/`
- `public/assets/tiles/frlg/decoration/`

Extract same categories as Emerald but with FRLG palette (more saturated, Kanto style):
**ground/**: grass-light.png, grass-dark.png, grass-flower.png, dirt.png, sand.png, path.png, city-floor.png
**water/**: deep.png, shallow.png, edge-n.png, edge-s.png, edge-e.png, edge-w.png
**nature/**: tree.png, tree-large.png, bush.png, rock.png, fence-h.png, fence-v.png
**buildings/**: pokecenter.png (multi-tile), mart.png, house.png, gym.png
**decoration/**: flowers.png, sign.png, lamppost.png, mailbox.png

Use the same extraction script from US-001 (extend it for FRLG section).

If tileset download fails, create placeholder colored rectangles (16×16) using sharp:
- grass: #5b9e4a, water: #3388cc, dirt: #9b7653, etc.

userStories.title: Organize PMD tiles from pokemonAutoChess
userStories.description:
Organize tiles from the pokemonAutoChess PMD tilesets into the editor tile library.

Check `tmp-samples/` and `public/assets/tiles/pmd/` for already-downloaded PMD tilesets (LushPrairie, MystifyingForest, BeachCave, ForestPath).

If not downloaded, fetch from:
- `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/keldaanCommunity/pokemonAutoChess/master/app/public/src/assets/tilesets/LushPrairie/tileset.png`
- `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/keldaanCommunity/pokemonAutoChess/master/app/public/src/assets/tilesets/MystifyingForest/tileset.png`
- `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/keldaanCommunity/pokemonAutoChess/master/app/public/src/assets/tilesets/BeachCave/tileset.png`
- `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/keldaanCommunity/pokemonAutoChess/master/app/public/src/assets/tilesets/ForestPath/tileset.png`

PMD tileset format: 432×192px = 3 sections of 144×192px. Each section is 6×8 grid of 24×24 tiles.
- Section 1 (cols 0-5): primary terrain (floor+wall autotiles)
- Section 2 (cols 6-11): water tiles
- Section 3 (cols 12-17): alternate terrain

Key tile positions (within each 6×8 section): position (1,1) = center/pure fill tile (best for standalone use).

Create directories:
- `public/assets/tiles/pmd/lush-prairie/`
- `public/assets/tiles/pmd/mystifying-forest/`
- `public/assets/tiles/pmd/beach-cave/`
- `public/assets/tiles/pmd/forest-path/`

Extract per tileset (~4 tiles each, 24×24):
- floor-1.png — Section 1, position (1,1) = center fill
- floor-2.png — Section 3, position (1,1) = alternate fill
- water.png — Section 2, position (1,1) = water fill
- wall.png — Section 1, position (5,0) = full wall/obstacle

Use the extraction script (extend for PMD 24×24 grid).

userStories.title: Create tile registry and BootScene dynamic loader
MapEditorScene — grid canvas with pan and zoom
userStories.description:
Create `src/scenes/MapEditorScene.ts` — the core editor scene.

Register in Phaser game config (find where scenes are registered, add MapEditorScene).

Access: In SelectScene, detect password 'editor' (similar to 'lulu' for ShowcaseScene) to launch MapEditorScene. Or detect URL param `?editor=true`.

**Grid rendering:**
- Default size: 125×125 tiles, tile size 24px
- Render grid lines (thin gray, 0x333333, alpha 0.3)
- Fill each cell with the default tile (grassLight from Emerald theme)
- Only render tiles visible in the viewport (culling for performance)

**Camera controls:**
- Pan: WASD keys (move camera) + middle-mouse drag
- Zoom: scroll wheel, min 0.25x to max 4x, zoom toward cursor position
- Camera bounds: slightly larger than map to allow some overshoot

**Cursor:**
- Highlight tile under mouse cursor (yellow border, alpha 0.5)
- Show coordinates in corner: 'x: 42, y: 17'

**Map size:**
- Store as instance variables: mapWidth, mapHeight (in tiles)
- Store terrain data: `Map<string, string>` where key = 'col,row', value = biome/tile ID

**Background:** Dark gray (0x1a1a1a) outside the map area.

Must compile with `npx tsc --noEmit`.

userStories.title: Editor palette panel (right side)
userStories.description:
Add the palette panel to MapEditorScene on the right side of the screen.

The palette is a UI overlay (fixed to camera, not part of the world).

Width: 200px fixed. Background: 0x222222, alpha 0.95.

**Layout (top to bottom):**

1. **Layer tabs** (3 radio buttons): Terreno | Objetos | Markers
   - Only Terreno active for now (Objetos and Markers show 'Em breve')

2. **Mode toggle** (Terreno layer only): [Biomas] [Tiles]
   - Biomas: shows 5 biome options (grassland, forest, lake, dirt_zone, path) as colored rectangles with labels
   - Tiles: shows grid of actual tile thumbnails

3. **Source tabs** (Tiles mode only): [Emerald] [FRLG] [PMD]
   - Filters tile grid by source

4. **Category filter** (Tiles mode only): [All] [Ground] [Water] [Nature] [Buildings] [Deco]

5. **Tile grid**: scrollable grid of tile thumbnails (40×40 each with 4px gap)
   - Each tile shows the actual texture scaled to fit
   - Selected tile has green border (0x00ff88, 2px)
   - Click to select

Use Phaser containers for each section. The palette should scroll vertically if content exceeds screen height.

Store selected tile/biome: `private selectedTileId: string | null` and `private selectedBiome: string | null`.

IMPORTANT: The palette must be rendered on a separate camera or using setScrollFactor(0) so it stays fixed while the map pans.

Must compile with `npx tsc --noEmit`.

userStories.title: Painting tools — brush, bucket, rectangle, eraser
userStories.description:
Implement painting tools in MapEditorScene.

**Toolbar** at the top of the screen (full width, 50px height, 0x2a2a2a background):
- Row 1: [Map: 125×125] [Expandir +] [Gerar Aleatório]
- Row 2: Tool buttons: [Pincel 1×1 ▼] [Balde] [Retângulo] [Borracha]
- Pincel dropdown: 1×1, 3×3, 5×5 brush sizes

Toolbar is fixed (setScrollFactor(0)), above the map canvas.

**Tool implementations:**

1. **Brush (Pincel)**: On left-click, paint selected tile/biome at cursor position.
   - Size 1×1: single tile
   - Size 3×3: 9 tiles centered on cursor
   - Size 5×5: 25 tiles centered on cursor
   - Support click-drag to paint continuously

2. **Bucket (Balde)**: Flood fill from cursor position. Replace all connected tiles of the same type with selected tile/biome. Use BFS with max 10000 tiles limit to prevent freeze.

3. **Rectangle (Retângulo)**: Click start corner, drag to end corner, release to fill rectangle area with selected tile/biome. Show preview rectangle while dragging.

4. **Eraser (Borracha)**: Left-click removes tile from terrain map (reverts to grassland default). Same brush size options as Pincel.

**Right-click**: Always erases (shortcut), regardless of selected tool.

**Visual feedback**: Tool cursor changes based on selected tool:
- Brush: shows brush size outline
- Bucket: crosshair
- Rectangle: crosshair
- Eraser: X mark

Store tool state: `private currentTool: 'brush' | 'bucket' | 'rectangle' | 'eraser'` and `private brushSize: 1 | 3 | 5`.

After painting, update the visible tile sprites immediately.

Must compile with `npx tsc --noEmit`.

userStories.title: Undo/Redo system
userStories.description:
Implement undo/redo for the map editor.

**Data structure:**
```typescript
interface EditorAction {
  readonly changes: ReadonlyArray<{ col: number; row: number; oldValue: string | undefined; newValue: string | undefined }>;
}
```

Each paint operation (single brush stroke, bucket fill, rectangle fill) creates ONE EditorAction with ALL tile changes in that operation.

**Stack:**
- `undoStack: EditorAction[]` (max 100 entries)
- `redoStack: EditorAction[]`
- On new action: push to undoStack, clear redoStack
- On undo: pop from undoStack, apply oldValues, push to redoStack
- On redo: pop from redoStack, apply newValues, push to undoStack

**Keybindings:**
- Ctrl+Z: Undo
- Ctrl+Y (or Ctrl+Shift+Z): Redo

**UI:** Show undo/redo buttons in toolbar (grayed out when stack is empty).

After undo/redo, update visible tile sprites.

Must compile with `npx tsc --noEmit`.

userStories.title: Export, import, and auto-save
userStories.description:
Implement the bottom bar with 4 buttons + auto-save.

**Bottom bar**: Full width, 50px height, 0x2a2a2a background, fixed (setScrollFactor(0)).

Buttons:
1. **Salvar Local**: Save to localStorage key `map-editor:{mapName}`
2. **Copiar JSON**: Copy compact JSON to clipboard using `navigator.clipboard.writeText()`
3. **Baixar JSON**: Trigger file download using Blob + URL.createObjectURL + anchor click
4. **Carregar**: Show overlay listing saved maps from localStorage. Click to load.

**JSON format** (as defined in design doc):
```json
{
  "name": "Map Name",
  "version": 1,
  "width": 125,
  "height": 125,
  "tileSize": 24,
  "terrain": { "col,row": "tileId or biomeId", ... },
  "objects": [],
  "markers": []
}
```

`terrain` only stores non-grassland cells (compaction).

**Auto-save**: Every 30 seconds, auto-save to localStorage key `map-editor:__autosave__`. Show brief flash text 'Salvo!' when auto-save triggers.

**Map name**: Editable text field in toolbar. Default: 'Novo Mapa'. Used in JSON `name` field and localStorage key.

**Load flow**: On scene start, check for auto-save. If exists, ask 'Continuar editando?' with Yes/No.

Must compile with `npx tsc --noEmit`.

userStories.title: Random map generation button
userStories.description:
Add 'Gerar Aleatório' button to toolbar that fills the editor canvas with procedural noise.

Reuse the noise functions from WorldSystem: `hash()`, `valueNoise()`, `fbm()`, `pickBiomeHint()`.

Since these are private methods on WorldSystem, extract them into a shared utility:
1. Create `src/utils/map-noise.ts` with the 4 functions as standalone exports
2. Refactor WorldSystem to import and use these functions instead of its private methods
3. MapEditorScene imports the same functions

When button is clicked:
1. Confirm dialog: 'Gerar mapa aleatório? Isso apaga o mapa atual.' (Phaser overlay with Yes/No)
2. Clear current terrain data
3. For each cell (col, row), call `pickBiomeHint(col, row, mapWidth, mapHeight)`
4. Convert BiomeHint to biome IDs: grassLight→null (default), grassDark→'grassDark', grassFlower→'grassFlower', tree→'forest', rock→'rock', dirt→'dirt_zone', water→'lake', waterEdge→'lake', path→'path'
5. Store non-null values in terrain map
6. Refresh all visible tiles
7. Clear undo/redo stacks

Must compile with `npx tsc --noEmit`.

userStories.title: Mini-map
userStories.description:
Add mini-map to the bottom-right corner of the editor, above the bottom bar.

Size: 150×150px. Background: 0x111111. Border: 1px 0x444444.
Position: fixed (setScrollFactor(0)), bottom-right with 10px margin.

**Rendering:**
- Create a RenderTexture (150×150)
- Each tile in the map = 1-2 pixels on the mini-map
- Color by biome/tile type:
  - grassland/grassLight: #5b9e4a
  - forest/tree: #2d5a1e
  - lake/water: #3388cc
  - dirt_zone/dirt: #9b7653
  - path: #c4a882
  - grassDark: #4e8c40
  - grassFlower: #7bb85a
  - rock: #888888

**Viewport indicator:**
- Draw white rectangle showing the current camera viewport on the mini-map
- Update position every frame

**Click to navigate:**
- Click on mini-map to center main camera on that position

Refresh mini-map when terrain changes (debounced, max 1 refresh per 500ms).

Must compile with `npx tsc --noEmit`.

userStories.title: WorldSystem JSON map loading
Expand map size option in editor
userStories.description:
Add 'Expandir' button to the toolbar that lets the user increase map size.

Current default: 125×125 tiles.

When clicked, show a Phaser overlay with:
- Current size: '125 × 125'
- Width input: number (min 50, max 500)
- Height input: number (min 50, max 500)
- [Aplicar] [Cancelar] buttons

On apply:
1. If new size > current: expand map, keeping existing terrain data. New cells default to grassland.
2. If new size < current: show warning 'Tiles fora do novo tamanho serão perdidos'. On confirm, trim terrain data.
3. Recreate grid visuals
4. Update mini-map
5. Add to undo stack (old size → new size, with trimmed tiles if shrunk)

Also update the map size display in toolbar: 'Map: {w}×{h}'

Must compile with `npx tsc --noEmit`.
